text.title: error-handling-in-vui Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for error-handling-in-vui as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLM-driven VUI bypasses the fixed re-prompt ladder because the model improvises responses.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Realtime API and Gemini Live models improvise and do not follow rung-by-rung fallback. Use system-prompt rules to enforce escalation triggers; test that the tool call actually fires, not just that the model says "transferring you".

failure-mode.description: Example phrases on rung 2 that do not exist in the NLU training data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every rung-2 example must round-trip through the recognizer. An invented example phrase trains users to say something the NLU cannot recognize, generating a guaranteed no-match on the retry.

failure-mode.description: Apologetic templates that stack "I'm sorry" across multiple rungs.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Subagents writing re-prompt copy default to apologetic patterns. A single apology on a system error is appropriate; stacking "I'm sorry" across rungs signals the system is broken rather than helping the user recover.

failure-mode.description: Re-prompts that use identical phrasing across rungs (stuck-loop pattern).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Users interpret repeated identical prompts as a system freeze. Vary phrasing across rungs to signal progression through the recovery sequence.
